observations.recommendation:
1.22. We recommended that the City Mayor:
1.22.1 File request of authority from COA with the required supporting
documents to derecognize non-existing/missing PPE items and
unserviceable properties;
1.22.2 Require the City Accountant Designate:
1.22.2.1 Record accounting entries for the recognition of PPE
found at station, if any, derecognition of lost/ non-
existing/missing PPEs, writing off/dropping from the
books of accounts of the remaining amounts lumped
under the unreconciled subsidiary ledgers of PPEs,
when authorized; and
1.22.2.2 Present the necessary disclosures in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.
1.22.2.3 To stop the practice of directly adding to the PPE all
partial payments of infrastructure projects, instead,
follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009
on debiting the cost of these uncompleted projects
to Construction in Progress account to present fairly the
yearend balance of each PPE item.
1.22.2.4 To prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop
from the books all infrastructure projects which were
duly transferred and acknowledged receive by the
recipients and end-user Barangays and see to it that
these are properly recorded in the Barangay books.
1.22.2.5 To drop from the LGU books the carrying amount of
all unserviceable properties, as soon as the authority to
derecognize is granted by appropriate office to
bring the reported balance of the PPE account in the
financial statements in order.

2.11. We recommended that the City Mayor:
2.11.1 Require the City Accountant Designate to issue demand letters to the
Barangays whose financial assistance remain unliquidated 30 days
after the date of project completion as stipulated in provision no. 5 of
the MOA;
2.11.2 Require the City Engineer to submit to the City Mayor and City
Accountant a report on the status of implementation of the projects
with details as to the actual date of completion to serve as basis on the
grant of additional funds to the Barangays. This should be done on a
regular basis or as required in the MOA;
2.11.3 Approve fund transfers by tranches so that the succeeding tranche be
given only after the previous fund transfer has been liquidated. A
certification from the City Accountant that the funds previously
transferred to the Barangay have been liquidated and accounted for in
the books shall be prepared for this purpose, and;
2.11.4 Recall or cancel the funds transferred to Barangays, unless by force
majeure, should they fail to implement the projects within three
months from receipt of funds, as specified in provision no. 6 of the
MOA.

3.7 We recommended that the City Mayor:
3.7.1 Direct the Acting GS Officer to see to it that all properties owned by
the City are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS as
mandated by RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and
3.7.2 Ensure that the insurance premiums of these properties are included
in the proposed Annual Budget.

4.10 We recommended that:
4.10.1 The City Treasurer requires suppliers to issue Sales Invoices or Official
Receipts, whichever is appropriate, with complete details and in the
correct amount corresponding to the payment received; and
4.10.2 The City Accountant- Designate submit to the Audit Team the lacking
Charges Invoices from the Suppliers concerned for the transactions
enumerated above, otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in
audit and subsequently be disallowed if not complied within the
reglementary period.

5.11. We recommended that the City DRRM Officer submit justification to the
Audit Team for non-inclusion in the DRRM FIP the PPAs charged against the
unexpended DRRM Fund of prior years.
5.12. Henceforth, we recommended that the DRRM Council to include in the annual
Local DRRM Fund Investment Plan, under a separate caption, the list of
projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended prior years’ Local
DRRM Fund.

6.7.1 Stop charging from the Local DRRM Fund expenses not related to
disaster and risk management activities, instead, assess the adequacy
and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the Local
DRRM Office and prioritize the procurement of these equipment;
6.7.2 Revisit the above-mentioned guidelines on the utilization of Local
DRRM Fund before charging certain expenditures against the said
fund; and
6.7.3 Submit justification and legal basis on the grant of P274,000.00 in cash
to the Punong Barangays out of the Local DRRM Fund.

8.21 We recommended that the Western Leyte Provincial Hospital and City Heath
Office personnel as well as the BHWs and the Local Health Board submit the
lacking supporting documents such as records, orders, etc. to the Audit Team
to support payments of AHDP and SRA and accordingly observe compliance
on the documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives to
avoid suspension in audit.
8.22 We also recommend that, henceforth, the City Accountant shall ensure that
all necessary supporting documents are attached to these claims before
payments are made.

8.10 We recommended that the City Mayor require the Punong Barangays to
submit the Distribution List showing the names /signatures of the recipients,
the items and quantity received.
8.11 Also, we recommended that the City Accountant submit justification why
payments were approved despite the absence of the required supporting
documents. Henceforth, we recommend that due diligence shall be exercised
in the review of documentary requirements prior to the approval of
disbursement vouchers.

1.11. We recommended that the:
a) Officer-In-Charge (OIC)-City Accountant re-compute the accumulated
depreciation of all depreciable assets and prepare an adjusting journal
entry to set the accounts in order. Henceforth, provide for the monthly
depreciation for the subject properties.
b) Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all the properties of the
City and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with Items 6.1 and 6.2 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006.
c) GSO in coordination with the Accounting Department:
1. Reconcile its property records with those of the RPCPPE in accordance
with the provisions of Items 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
2. Follow the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs,
pursuant to pertinent provisions in Item 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-
006.
1.12. The OIC-City Accountant:
a) After completing the reconciliation procedures, establish the reconciled
balances for each PPE account as the correct balance of PPE at year end,
b) Record accounting entries for the recognition of PPE found at station, if
any, recognition of loss of PPE, writing-off/dropping from the books of
accounts the remaining balances amounts lumped under the unreconciled
Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) of PPEs, and derecognition of non-
existing/missing items; and
c) Present the necessary disclosures on PPE in the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

2.14. We recommended that the:
a. City Mayor create Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical count
of all inventory items including items received but not yet paid as of June
30 and December 31; and ensure that the Inventory Committee prepare
and submit to the City Accountant and the Audit Team, the consolidated
RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, to confirm
quantities on hand.
b. City Supply Officer prepare the consolidated Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI)/RSMI (as currently used by the City) every 15th
day of the month, duly supported with the RIS and SSMI/RSMI received
from the requisitioning offices and submit the consolidated SSMI/RSMI to
the GSO not later than the 20th day of the month.
c. General Service Officer review the consolidated SSMI/RSMI received
from the Supply Officer and submit the same to the City Accountant within
five days from receipt thereof, instead of submitting its own version of
SSMI/RSMI;
d. The City Accountant:
1. Record and classify the supplies procured by the City in the
appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, including items
received but not yet paid as of year end;
2. Record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt
of the consolidated SSMI/RSMI from the GSO supported with the RIS
and RSMI of the requisitioning offices or end-users;
3. Update each item of inventory in the Supply Ledger Card, showing its
receipts, issuances and balances;
4. Apply the weighted average method in determining costs of each item
of inventories;
5. Reconcile the semestral RPCI of the GSO with the balances of
Inventories in the books; and
6. Investigate and identify any difference in balances, and adjust
accordingly.

2.7 We recommended that the City Mayor issue a memorandum order for the
General Services Officer, City Assessor and City Legal Officer to cause the
registration and transfer of title of the lands purchased in the name of the City
and that the City Assessor make representation with the Sangguniang
Panlungsod to appropriate funds for this purpose.

4.9. We recommended that the City Mayor:
1. Submit to the Audit Team an explanation/justification, for using the
BGCM for PPAs other than those enumerated under LBC No. 125.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted; and
2. Ensure that the remaining BGCM fund be utilized exclusively for
the PPAs and expenses enumerated under LBC No. 125.

5.8. We recommended that the CAO prepare a monthly report on fund utilization
and Status of Implementation of PPAs using the format prescribed in Annex
C of DBM LBC No. 125, as amended by Annex A of DBM Circular Letter No.
2020-10 and comply with the requirement of posting the report to the City’s
website and in at least three conspicuous places.

6.7. We recommended that the City Treasurer require the suppliers to issue Sales
Invoice or Official Receipts on the subject transactions to properly
substantiate the payment made to them by the City in order to oblige the
Suppliers to declare the income from these transactions for tax purposes.

7.18. We recommended that the CDRRM Officer:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the certified list of recipients with the
corresponding designations, office/area of assignment, number of hours on
duty per day and actual work relative to COVID-19 activities;
b. Submit to Audit Team justifications or explanations on the above-noted
observations. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Disallowance, if warranted.
7.19. We likewise recommended that for similar transactions in the future, the
CDRRM Officer:
1. Coordinate with BAC and GSO in the preparation of detailed PPMP for
food supplies to be submitted to BAC for consolidation to the APP, and
record/update the corresponding SCs;
2. Submit the list of recipients of meals per day with the corresponding area
of assignment/location;
3. Prepare a daily menu with its corresponding budget and the number of
persons to be served per meal as basis for the procurement of
supplies/ingredients; and
4. Ensure that food packs are given only to personnel directly involved in the
implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs.
7.20. We also recommended that the OIC-City Accounting Office check the
completeness and veracity of all documents supporting the disbursement and
require the CDRRMO to submit lacking documents prior to payment.

8.15. We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Submit a written explanation categorically stating the legal basis for the
payment of the incentives, supported with an appropriation ordinance
consistent with law, authorizing the payment of the same.
b) Ensure that payment of similar kind of incentive is backed with a statutory
authorization and appropriation ordinance authorizing the payment.
8.16. We also recommended that the Officer-in-charge of the City Accounting
Office check the completeness and validity of supporting documents of the
expenditures.
8.17. For claimants employed in NGA who have not yet submitted the required
Accountant’s Certification mentioned above, we recommended the OIC-
Accountant require these personnel to submit the said Certification.
8.18. We further recommended that the City Treasurer instruct the Disbursing
Officer to stop the practice of giving the claims to persons other than the
payees appearing in the payroll.

9.17. We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) and the Local Development Council review or assess the City’s
implementation of the 20% Development PPAs and identify and thresh out
the causes of the low implementation;
b) in coordination with the City Planning and Development Office (CPDO)
and the City Engineering Office, undertake measures to eliminate
procurement backlogs and address the risk of significant delays in the
procurement of infrastructure projects to ensure that all projects funded
by the 20% DF including the fund transfers to the barangays are
implemented within the timelines set by the Local Development Council.
Further, ensure that development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF
are well-planned and procurement-and implementation ready pursuant to
Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2020; and
c) the CPDO ensures that barangays who are recipient of 20% DF fund
transfers strictly observe the provisions in the MOA on the implementation
of projects and liquidation of funds received, otherwise barangays that fail
76
to comply with the MOA should not be given additional fund transfers.
9.18. Further, we recommended that the OIC of the Accounting Department prepares a
Quarterly Report on the Status of Liquidation of funds transferred to the barangays
and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
9.19. Lastly, we recommended that all savings derived from completed and
discontinued projects in prior years be reverted to the unappropriated surplus and
re-programmed to other projects subject to revision of the AIP and the enactment
by the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

10.13 We recommended that the CDRRMO and the CDRRM Council conduct
assessment on the identified possible disasters that the City may experience,
and prepare a more realistic LDRRMFIP with identified implementable PPAs
that would enhance the City’s capability and preparedness to respond
disasters and calamities, to be funded from prior years’ unexpended
LDRRMF.

11.13. We recommended that the City provides CDRRMO funding to improve the
storage rooms for the procurement of floor to ceiling racks/bins.
11.14. We also recommended that the CDRRMO:
a. Assign one room for food supplies, medicines and vitamins: one room for
DRRM equipment and supplies and one room for PPE & hygiene kits, and
housekeeping supplies;
b. See to it that procured items are piled separately from the donated items
to facilitate monitoring;
c. Provide markers/labels/tags for easy identification and retrieval of items;
d. Keep the storage facility adequately secured;
e. Maintain perpetual inventory records that will account the physical
movement for each inventory item indicating their running balances; and
f. Assign inventory in-charge for each shift who will control the inflow and
outflow of supplies and document said movement.
11.15. We also recommended that the GSO coordinates closely with the personnel-
in-charge of the inventories and with the OIC-City Accountant for the proper
monitoring and recording of inventories.

12.6. We recommended that the OIC-City Accountant establish the balances of all
unutilized PPAs and prepare the necessary registries as required in the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

13.15. We recommended that the City provides the CSWDO with sufficient,
accessible safe, secured and exclusive storeroom for food supplies inventories.
13.16. We also recommended the CSWDO:
a. Assign personnel who will be in-charge of the over-all management of the
storage room, including the maintenance of inventory records and ensure
that inventory items are safely stored and accounted for, including the
monitoring of expiry dates and regular inspection to check spoilage, among
others.
b. Adopts safety measures in the movement and repacking of inventories to
avoid spoilage.
c. Conduct random check/inspection to check whether the personnel in-
charge/s are performing their functions in the maintenance of records and
safekeeping of inventory items and to ensure the good status of the stocked
food supplies inventories.

14.8. We recommended that the City Mayor creates an Inventory Committee that
will conduct a physical count of all the remaining donated items.
14.9. We also recommended that the City Supply Officer:
a. prepare inventory list of the remaining donated items for DRRM every
month or every quarter if the City is under quarantine for more than three
months and determine the condition of these items; and
b. Henceforth, prepare all the reports and documents required to account
and monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and submit
a copy of all the reports to the Audit Team.

15.5. We recommended that the City Supply Officer furnish the City Accountant with
the duplicate copies of all Acknowledgment Receipts of in-kind donations,
invoice receipt and inventory list of donated items for DRRM.
15.6. We also recommended that the OIC-City Accountant summarize data from
the documents submitted by the City Supply Officer according to the required
disclosure, compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for
DRRM maintained by the City Supply Officer for check and balance, and
disclose the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

16.9. We reiterated part of last year’s audit recommendation, with modification, to
restore the retention money to the DBP building fund account from whatever
legal source that may be authorized by the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

We recommended that the Accountant and the Head of GSO: 1. retrieve previous accounting records to identify the PPEs comprising the unaccounted PPEs; 2. conduct a detailed thorough physical count per office and include identification of buildings owned by the City of Baybay, and; 3. Restore the amount of PPEs derecognized in the books without legal basis.
observations.managementAction:
JEV has been restoring Implemented
GSO made restoring Implemented
will properly
dispose whatever is
disposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: A. The designated Custodians of each requisitioning office: 4. Prepare the RIS when supplies are issued to end-users/patients; 5. Prepare, and submit to the CSO every 5th day of the month, supported with the RIS in numerical sequence; 6. Maintain an updated stock card; 7. Conduct physical count of inventory items by type every June 30 and December 31 every year; and 8. Prepare, and submit to the City Supply Officer, the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories every 15th day of July and January each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, to confirm balances on hand. B. The City Supply Officer: 9. Prepare and submit to the City Accountant the consolidated SSMI every 15th day of the month, duly supported with the RIS and SSMI from the requisitioning offices, and; 10. Prepare the consolidated RPCI and submit to the City Accountant and Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, to confirm balances on hand. C. The City Accountant: 11. Record the supplies procured by the City Government in the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase; 12. Record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt of the consolidated SSMI from the CSO supported with the RIS and SSMI of the requisitioning office or end-users; and 13. Reconcile the semestral RPCI of the CSO with the balances of Inventories in the books. We also recommended that the City Budget Officer require all Department Heads to use the appropriate expense accounts in the preparation of their respective budgets and stop providing lump sum appropriations for Other MOOE.

We recommended that: A. The City Accountant: 14. Record the revenues and receivable due from PHIC when billing statements are submitted to the said agency, and; 15. Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers for each agency debtor. B. The Revenue Collection Clerk (RCC) assigned at BCMDDC: 16. Maintain a record of every services rendered where running totals of collections and unpaid claims as of a given date could be generated, and; 17. Submit to the Accountant the monthly balances of unpaid claims.

We recommended that the City Accountant record immediately any valid variances identified during the reconciliation process between bank and book transactions before closing the books of accounts at year-end in order to reflect the year-end cash balance in the City’s year-end Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant: 19. identify the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2016; 20. record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; 21. maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; 22. prepare a lapsing schedule to compute and record the depreciation of each asset.

We recommended that the City Engineer and the Head of the General Services Office, in coordination with the City Accountant, reconcile their respective reports to confirm the balances in the accounting records.

We recommended that: 24. the City Accountant require the end-users of the procured inventory items to submit a report on its utilization and balances as basis in preparing the accounting entry to restore inventories in the books; and 25. record subsequent expenses in the appropriate accounts for accurate presentation in the books. 26. We also recommended that the City Budget Officer require all Department Heads to use the appropriate expense accounts in the preparation of their respective budgets and stop providing lump sum appropriations for Other MOOE.

We recommended that: 27. the BAC Chairman conduct the bidding of projects identified in the AIP, following the schedule of projects indicated in the PPMP; 28. the City Planning and Development Officer monitor the bidding of projects listed in the AIP by submitting a report to the City Mayor and the BAC on projects in which procurement are delayed, and; 29. the City Engineer implement and monitor strictly all projects awarded during the year to ensure its completion within the timelines specified in the contract agreement.
observations.reason: Not all the projects to be funded listed in the AIP were implemented as plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Engineer: 30. identify the causes of the delay in the implementation of the 13 projects; 31. if due to contractors’ fault, impose the necessary liquidated damages; and 32. rescind contracts on projects which has not been started in which the contract period has already lapsed and forfeit the related Performance Security.
observations.reason: As of CY 2019 all the 13 projects were all completed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 33. the City Accountant: (1) Obtain the complete set of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) on all programs including the Work and financial Plans as guide in the disbursements, and; (2) Certify expenditures within the approved allocation in the MOA. 34. We also recommended that the City Agriculturist comply with the provisions of the MOA.
observations.reason: This is partly included in CY 2019 Audit Finding

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 35. Record the City’s transactions in the Trust Fund to the proper sub-accounts; 36. Reconcile the balances of the different ledgers of the Due To NGA sub-accounts and the Trial Balance; 37. Record in a single SL receipt of funds for a particular program and its related disbursements per source agency and per purpose, regardless of the number of tranches.

We recommended that in future biddings, the BAC, its Secretariat and TWG, review carefully and thoroughly the eligibility documents submitted by participating bidders so that any misrepresentations will be detected and only bidders who strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 are declared eligible. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance resulting from the review of documents on the unimplemented or delayed contracts awarded to the subject contractors.

We recommended that the City Accountant deduct only, in the computation of the employees’ taxable income, those allowed in the National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines so that the amounts due to National Government are accurately remitted.
We recommended that the City Mayor designate a collector at the CHO who should be authorized to issue Official Revenue Collection Receipts for every laboratory and dental patients.

We recommended that: 41. Identify the fund transfers wherein the intended programs/projects have been completed, terminated, cancelled or abandoned in order to identify which funds are due to be returned; 42. Prepare and update schedules on the composition of the Due to LGU and Due to NGA accounts to support the book balances. Prepare adjusting entries to reclassify recording of erroneous fund transfers. 43. We also recommended that the City Treasurer determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of the Source Agencies to return all unutilized amount. In case the source agency can no longer be identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the City Mayor: 44. conduct continuous ocular inspections at the barangay level to validate the actual use of the real properties; 45. update the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, the exact addresses and identity of declared owners, the exemptions on RPT, if any; 46. update continuously the City’s records particularly on the Tax Declarations issued and check if there are clerical errors due to General Revisions. Direct the City Treasurer to: 47. develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign program to intensify and maximize collection of RPT/SET Receivables; 48. deputize bonded Barangay Treasurers, to collect all taxes on real property located in the Barangay; 49. prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies and post quarterly the said list at the City Hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; 50. submit the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies, with a statement of the reasons for such non-payment, to the Sanggunian concerned on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection either by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action.
observations.reason:
Implemented
Partially Implemented
Implemented
Collections are made in the City Hall only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor execute a MOA with the recipient barangays for the assistance in kind extended to said barangays incorporating in the MOA the submission of requirements, such as proof of actual utilization of the materials for the intended projects within a reasonable time after receipt of the materials. The MOA should also require that the cost of materials granted by the City be recorded by the recipient barangays as additions to their assets.

1.9 We recommended that the City Budget Officer appropriate and obligate the
wages of Job Order Services under MOOE in the City’s Annual Budget.

1.10 We also recommended that the City Accountant record the disbursement of
wages of Job Order personnel under the appropriate General Services
accounts (ex. Environmental, Janitorial, Security or Other General Services)
of the MOOE to have the expenses properly presented in the books of the City.

2.12. We recommended that the Department Heads in charge of projects/programs
funded by National Government Agencies always submit to the City
Accountant the list of all equipment distributed, duly-acknowledged by each
recipient.

2.13. We also recommended that the City Accountant prepare the necessary
accounting entries to derecognize the assets transferred or distributed to the
Barangays and other organizations, upon receipt of the documents
acknowledging such transfer.

3.8 We recommended that the City Accountant re-classify the properties held for
rent from PPE accounts to Investment Property using the cost model.

4.15 We recommended that the City Mayor, through the Bids and Awards
Committee on the Disposal of Unserviceable Properties:
1. Submit justification why government properties were demolished without
complying with the necessary requirements/procedures for the demolition;
2. Submit documents on the appraisal made on the structures, pictures of the
structures prior to their demolition, duly filled-up Inventory and
Inspection Report, Report of Waste Materials and disposal procedures on
the salvageable materials; and other evidences on the procedures
undertaken before, during and after demolition;
3. Submit to the City Accountant the documents on demolition as basis to
derecognize the demolished structures; and,
4. Deposit to the account of the City the proceeds from the disposal of the
materials, if any.

5.13 We recommended that the Market Supervisor and the City Treasurer:
1. Maintain individual ledgers for each lessee and update the same monthly
to identify the months with unpaid rents; and,
2. Prepare the summary of unpaid rentals and surcharges every end of the
month and submit to the City Accountant for recording of appropriate
Receivables.

5.14 We also recommended that the City Accountant record the receivables and
provide for the necessary allowance for doubtful accounts. Likewise, monitor
the accrual of advance rentals and record necessary entries.

6.14 We recommended that the City Accountant:
1. Write confirmation letters to the source agencies to reconcile the balances
in the books;
2. Prepare a journal entry voucher to correct the erroneous recording of
funds received from sources other than Local Government Units and
other local collections;
3. Identify and determine if the City has existing valid payables from the
source agencies; confirm continuity of the programs/projects; determine
the availability of the equivalent cash of each source and return the
unused balances to the source agencies; and
4. Revert all unknown balances as income in the General Fund if supported
with cash.

6.15 We also recommended that, in future receipt of financial Assistance, the City
Treasurer and the Department Heads in charge with a program or project,
obtain a copy of the MOA for every fund transferred to the City and furnish
a copy thereof to the City Accountant as guide in determining the purpose and
in processing its disbursements.

7.12 We recommended that the City Accountant:
1. Trace back the transactions affecting the RPT/SET Receivables and
Deferred RPT Liability accounts to identify the causes of the variance of ₱2
million and arrive at the correct balances of these accounts;
2. Record the tax discount for prompt payment of RPT and the condoned tax
liabilities arising from the availment of Tax Amnesty Program; and,
3. Exclude Fines and Penalties as deductions from Tax Receivable and
Deferred Tax Income and record the same in the appropriate Income
account.

7.13We also recommended that the City Assessor:
1. Conduct physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real
properties within the City are properly listed in the assessment rolls;
2. Conduct vigorous Tax Mapping activities in order to resolve issues on
duplicate Tax Declarations; and,
3. Update and reconcile all records pertaining to real properties within the
City and the owners.

7.14 We further recommended that the City Treasurer:
1. Issue a notice of delinquency to the delinquent taxpayers;
2. Post the same in the main hall and in other publicly accessible and
conspicuous place of each barangay, and;
3. Implement other tax collection mechanisms and strategies to encourage
delinquent taxpayers to settle their tax liabilities and increase RPT
collections of the City;
4. In case of continued non-payment, prepare a certified list of all real
property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at
least one year including reasons for such non-collection; and,
5. Submit such list to the Sangguniang Panlungsod on or before December 31
of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies
were incurred, and request assistance from the SP in the enforcement of
the remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160.

8.12 We recommended that the City Mayor convene the City Development Council
so as to identify specific priority projects to be funded from the Continuing
Appropriations of ₱152 million and to have the current Annual Investment
Program revised to include the newly-identified projects. Any amount no
longer needed should be reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the City.

8.13 We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning
Development Officer (CPDO) to submit to the Local Finance Committee prior
to budget preparation, the implemented projects and the balances of the
appropriations for further review and possible re-programming and inclusion
in the annual budget.

9.12 We recommended that the City Mayor, through the City Planning
Development Officer, specify the projects to be implemented or goods to be
procured from its current year budget for Capital Outlay. Otherwise, secure
authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod prior to entering into a contract
when a project is not included or specifically provided in the City’s
appropriation ordinance to avoid legal repercussion.

10.16 We recommended that the City Budget Officer:
1. Obligate the total amount of approved contracts, whether or not the
contractor requested progress billing;
2. Monitor all obligations made on the Continuing Appropriations to avoid
unauthorized use of the funds; and
3. Review the balances in the Continuing Appropriations during the regular
annual budget preparation, revert those without specific purpose and re-
appropriate only when PPAs are identified in the AIPs.

10.17 We also recommended that the City Mayor specify the projects to be
undertaken from the appropriations on Capital Outlay of the CMO and other
City Departments, otherwise, obtain authority from the Sangguniang
Panlungsod, through a Supplemental Budget, whenever any portion thereof
will be utilized.

11.13 We recommended that the City Mayor and the members of the City
Development Council assess the attainability and necessity for the continuance
of the unimplemented projects and consider re-programming, if warranted.
Also, include in the AIP only the projects which can be readily implemented
during the year.

11.14 We further recommended that the City Engineer submit immediately to the
BAC Secretariat the Program of Work of all projects identified in the AIP
especially those funded in CY 2018 and prior years so that the same can be
scheduled immediately for bidding.

12.8 We recommended that the Budget Officer stop using the Continuing
Appropriations of the 20% Development Fund for projects which were not
previously included in the AIP, unless a specific authority therefor is issued by
the Sangguniang Panlungsod, and a revised AIP has been prepared.

13.12 We recommended that the City Engineer:
1. Submit to COA the written Order of the City Mayor to the contractor to
suspend the construction of the new City Hall, duly received and dated by
the contractor, indicating therein the reasons for such suspension, the date
of effectivity and the number of days of suspension; and
2. Prepare and submit the evaluation/assessment report on the
accomplishments of the contractor with regard to the project, as required
by the City Mayor, as of the date the work on the project was suspended,
with his (City Engineer) recommendations for actions to be taken by
management to ensure completion of the construction.

14.17 We recommended that the City Mayor, City Budget Officer, City Treasurer
and the City Accountant ensure that the ₱17,831,962.10 taxes remitted to BIR
from GF be replaced, the ₱2,107,844.33 business income be recorded and
remitted to the GF and the ₱28,014,591.05 retention money be restored to the
DBP building fund account, either from the subsequent loan release or from
whatever legal source that may be authorized by the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

15.14 We recommended that the CDRRM Officer:
1. Consult and discuss with the City’s DRRM Council the contents of the
City’s LDRRMFIP for evaluation as to the viability and continuity of the
projects/programs/activities listed therein and to revise the LDRRMFIP
indicating the 30% QRF in lump sum, showing the details of the 70%
Mitigation Fund and prior years’ balance transferred to the Special Trust
Fund;
2. Prepare a duly filled-out Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP)
of the PAPs for LDRRMF for consolidation with the APP of the City; and
3. Submit a quarterly report to the City Mayor on any unimplemented PAPs
in the LDRRMFIP and ensure maximum utilization of the DRRM fund.

16.12 We recommended that the City Mayor:
1. Compel the City Engineer and other department heads in charge with
ongoing projects to fast track their completion; or evaluate its viability,
revise plans/designs, where necessary and submit changes to the source
agencies concerned for approval to guarantee the immediate, continuous
and full implementation of the projects; and
2. Require the project/program in charge to submit Utilization Reports or
status reports to the City Accountant for the latter to determine the
remaining amounts from each project or program.

16.13 We also recommended that the City Accountant:
1. Provide the City Mayor with the list of balances of funds received from
NGAs for discussion and confirmation with the Department Heads in
charge of each project/program;
2. Prepare Disbursement Vouchers for the Bureau of Treasury for the
remittance of funds downloaded to the City in prior years which had been
dormant for several years;
3. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous
classification of funds received from PhilHealth and determine its purpose;
and
4. Reclassify the ₱2,190,479.92 received from PhilHealth to Trust Liabilities.

17.6 We recommended that the City Mayor through the City Engineer:
1. Issue the Certificate of Acceptance one year after completion of the project
or after the defects liability period;
2. Require the contractors to post Warranty Security upon the issuance of
Certificate of Acceptance and upon the release of retention money; and
3. Attach the Certificate of Acceptance to the Disbursement Vouchers for the
release of the Retention Fees.

18.9 We recommended that the City Mayor:
1. Through the BAC, hire consultants through competitive bidding and
resort to alternative modes only when the conditions in Rule XVI of the
2016 RIIR of RA No. 9184 are present;
2. Require the Human Services and Management Officer to review the
staffing pattern and service requirements in the organizational structure
of the City, assess the necessity of hiring consultants and render a report
thereon to the City Mayor. Consultants to be hired should not perform the
functions of regular employees and/or department heads; and
3. Use the HRMO review and assessment report as guide in the inclusion of
particular consultancy services in the annual PPMP and APP of the City.

19.7 We recommended that the City Mayor convene, and require, the Local
Finance Committee to:
1. Revisit and update the Revenue Code and the Market Code of Baybay City
specifically the rates, fees and charges to be imposed by the City;
2. Compute the appropriate rates for each market section including the new
Sections;
3. Provide for clear-cut procedures in enforcing delinquencies and other
amendments that will remedy and help improve market operations, and;
4. Submit the proposed revisions to the Sangguniang Panlungsod, with the
City Mayor’s recommending approval.

19.8 We also recommended that the City Vice Mayor, as the Presiding Officer of
the Sangguniang Panlungsod, facilitate the adoption of local revenue ordinance on the
revised rates and charges of the City.

19.9 Further, we recommended that the City Treasurer, through the Market
Administrator, collect the unpaid rentals, impose the necessary surcharges
and strictly enforce the provisions in the Market Code and the New Revenue
Code and any amendments thereto.

20.6 We recommended that the Market Administrator prepare and submit the list
of actual occupants of the market stalls to the City Legal Officer for
preparation of contracts of lease and for signature of the City Mayor and the
qualified stallholders.

21.9 We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Treasurer delegate and
assign as collectors only the permanent RCCs who are properly bonded.
RCCs should not be allowed to delegate their functions to Job Order
personnel.

22.12 We recommended that the City Mayor monitor strictly the planning and
execution of all GAD-related activities as indicated in the City’s annual GAD
Plan and Budget.

22.13 We also recommended for the GAD Focal Point System to:
1. Implement GAD-related activities promptly, effectively and efficiently in
accordance with the GPB; and
2. Obtain from the CPDO the specific list/name of PPAs with the
corresponding amount and location thereof attributed to GAD before the
formulation of the GPB and the preparation of GAD AR to ensure the
accuracy of the data acquired for evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the City Mayor execute a MOA with the recipient barangays for assistance in kind extended to said barangays incorporating in the MOA the submission of requirements, such as proof of actual utilization of the materials for the intended projects within a reasonable time after receipt of the materials. The MOA should also require that the cost of materials granted by the City be recorded by the recipient barangays as additions to their assets.

12. We recommended that the City Accountant and the Head of  the  GSO:  (1)
    restore the amount of PPEs derecognized  in  the  books  without  legal
    basis; (2) retrieve previous accounting records to  identify  the  PPEs
    comprising the unaccounted PPEs, and; (3) conduct a  detailed  thorough
    physical count per office  and  include  identification  of  lands  and
    buildings owned by the City of Baybay, in coordination  with  the  City
    Assessor’s Office.

19. We recommended that:

    A. The designated Custodian of each requisitioning office:


    (a)  Prepare the RIS when supplies are issued to end-users/patients;
    (b)  Prepare, and submit to the City Supply Officer, the SSMI every 5th
    day of
        the month, supported with the RIS in numerical sequence;
    (c)  Maintain an updated stock card;
    (d) Conduct physical count of inventory items by type every June 30 and
        December 31 every year; and
    (e)  Prepare, and submit to the City Supply Officer, the Report on  the
        Physical Count of Inventories every 15th day  of  July  and  January
        each year.

    B. The City Supply Officer:


     f) Prepare the consolidated SSMI every 15th  day  of  the  month,  duly
        supported with the RIS and SSMI  received  from  the  requisitioning
        offices and submit the consolidated SSMI to the GSO not  later  than
        the 20th day of the month.

    C. The General Service Officer


     g) Review the consolidated SSMI received from the City  Supply  Officer
        and submit the same to  the  City  Accountant  within  5  days  from
        receipt thereof; and
     h) Prepare,  and  submit  to  the  City  Accountant  and  Auditor,  the
        consolidated RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
        for  the  first  and  second  semesters,  respectively,  to  confirm
        balances on hand and as reference in the preparation of  Procurement
        Plan.

     D. The City Accountant:


     i)  Record  the  supplies  procured  by  the  City  Government  in  the
        appropriate Inventory account upon purchase;
     j) Record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt
        of the consolidated SSMI from the GSO supported  with  the  RIS  and
        SSMI of the requisitioning offices or end-users; and
     k) Reconcile the semestral  RPCI  of  the  GSO  with  the  balances  of
        Inventories in the books.

7. We recommended that:

    A. The Revenue Collection Clerk (RCC) assigned at BCMDDC:
        a. Maintain a record of every services rendered where running totals
           of collections and unpaid claims as of  a  given  date  could  be
           generated based on the Statement of Account (SOA) per patient;
        b. Prepare a summary  report  of  unpaid  balance  of  the  services
           rendered; and
        c. Submit to the Accountant the monthly balances of unpaid claims.
    B. The City Accountant:
        d. Record the revenues and receivable due from Philhealth, DSWD  and
           City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) upon receipt of the SOA, and;
        e. Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers for each agency debtor.

7. We recommended that the City Accountant record  immediately  any  valid
    variances identified during the reconciliation process between bank and
    book transactions before closing the books of accounts at  year-end  in
    order to reflect the correct year-end cash balance in the City’s  year-
    end Financial Statements.

9. We recommended that the City Accountant:

    a. Record the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2016
       to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts;
    b. Maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure; and
    c.    Prepare a lapsing schedule to compute and record the depreciation
       of each asset.

8. We recommended that the City Engineer and the General Services Officer,
     in coordination with the City Accountant,  reconcile  their  respective
     reports to confirm the balances in the accounting records.

7. We  recommended  that  the  City  Accountant  record  expenses  in  the
    appropriate accounts for accurate presentation in the books.

  8. We also recommended that the City Budget Officer require all Department
    Heads to use the appropriate expense accounts  in  the  preparation  of
    their respective budgets and stop providing lump sum appropriations for
    Other MOOE.

11.  We recommended that:

    a)  The  City  Development  Council  (1)  evaluate  first  the  projects
       recommended by the Barangay Chairpersons before the said projects
       are incorporated in the City’s Annual Investment Plan; (2) exclude
       in the City’s AIP procurements that should be paid from the MOOE;


    b) The City Planning and Development Officer (1) submit a report to  the
       City Mayor and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)  of  the  list  of
       projects  with  delayed  procurement;  (2)  submit   to   the   Local
       Development Council the list of projects with duplication  and  those
       no longer needed  to  be  implemented  so  that  the  budget  can  be
       reprogrammed;


    c) The City Engineer implement and monitor strictly all projects awarded
       during the year to  ensure  their  completion  within  the  timelines
       specified in the contract/agreement; and


    d) The BAC Chairman conduct the bidding of projects  identified  in  the
       AIP, following  the  schedule  of  implementation  indicated  in  the
       Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP).

9. We recommended that the City Engineer:

     1) Impose the necessary liquidated damages on the contractors;
     2) Rescind contracts on projects which have not been started and  which
        the contract period had already lapsed; and
     3) Forfeit the related Performance Security.

10. We recommended that the City Accountant: (1) Obtain the  complete  set
      of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) on all programs  including  the  Work
      and Financial  Plans  as guide in the disbursements,  (2)  Certify
      expenditures within the approved  allocation  in  the  MOA,  and;  (3)
      Return  unused  funds  on  completed  projects  based  on   the   Fund
      Utilization Report (FUR).

  11. We also recommended  that  the  City  Agriculturist  comply  with  the
      provisions of the MOA. Also, submit to the Audit  Team  the  authority
      from the DA to utilize  the  savings  to  offset  against  the  excess
      expenditures, otherwise, the excess  amount  shall  be  disallowed  in
      audit.

13. We recommended that the City Accountant:

     1. Record the City’s transactions in the Trust Fund to the proper  sub-
        accounts;
     2. Reconcile the balances of the different ledgers of the  Due  to  NGA
        sub-accounts and the Trial Balance;
     3. Record in a single SL receipt of funds for a particular program  and
        its  related  disbursements  per  source  agency  and  per  purpose,
        regardless of the number of tranches.

8. We recommended that in future biddings, the BAC, its  Secretariat  and
      TWG,  review  carefully  and  thoroughly  the  eligibility   documents
      submitted by the participating bidders so that  any  misrepresentation
      will be detected and only the bidders who  strictly  comply  with  the
      provisions of RA No. 9184 are declared eligible.  This  recommendation
      is without  prejudice  to  the  issuance  of  Notice  of  Disallowance
      resulting from the review of documents on the unimplemented or delayed
      contracts awarded to the subject contractors.

3. We recommended that the City Accountant deduct, in the computation of
      the employees’ taxable income, those allowed in the National  Internal
      Revenue Code of the Philippines so that the  amounts  due  to  National
      Government are accurately remitted.

1. We recommended that the City Mayor designate a collector  at  the  CHO
      who should be bonded and authorized to  issue  Official  Receipts  for
      every laboratory and dental fees paid by CHO patients.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the necessary correcting entries to record the projects in the appropriate asset accounts in order to present the correct balance of the assets of the City.  If the City Government intends to donate such assets, the same may be transferred to the recipient barangays upon their acceptance, supported with the necessary documents to effect their transfer from the books of the City Government to the recipient barangays.

We recommended that the City Accountant identify and evaluate all differences existing between the records of the City and its depository bank.  Also, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the errors affecting the Cash-in-Bank account to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and communicate to the bank any errors made by it, if any.

We recommended that the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant obligate and record a transaction as accounts payable only when it is founded on a valid claim and properly supported with sufficient evidence such as, but not limited to, approved Disbursement Voucher, Purchase Order, Contracts, etc.

We further recommended that the City Accountant perform the following:

Supervise closely the accounting staff and instruct them to follow strictly the guidelines in recording the transactions to avoid blind certifications. 

prepare the list of Accounts Payable that has been dormant for two years or more and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in order to reflect the correct balance of the account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant trace back the unreconciled amount, conduct periodic monitoring of the payable accounts and designate accounting personnel dedicated to maintaining an updated monthly schedule of payables to support the amount reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant record depreciation of an asset starting when it is available for use.  Also, prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to correct the Equity and Accumulated Depreciation accounts to reflect the accurate balances of the affected accounts in the agency’s year-end financial statements.

observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

The process is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council identify specific projects to be funded from the 20% DF, including the use of continuing appropriations, based on priority needs of the City.  Also, stop lump sum appropriations as well as frequent augmentations, so that projects in the AIP of the City will be implemented fully within the year.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the Disbursement Voucher immediately to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF to the appropriate account under the Trust Fund in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 to safeguard the resources against possible misuse/misappropriation.

We recommended that the City Mayor, through the CDRRMO, utilize the Mitigation Fund of its LDRRMF solely for disaster- and calamity related purposes only.  This is without prejudiced to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately with the latter any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS to tally with the City records on premium contributions.

We also recommended that the City’s Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) furnish monthly all payroll preparers with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.  

Lastly, we recommended that the City Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the City Treasurer issue checks for the barangays’ share of RPTs in the names of the Barangays concerned to ensure that such monies will be deposited to the accounts of the barangays.

We recommended that the LSB incorporate in the SEF budget programs for health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.

We recommended that the City Accountant: 
 
Ensure that the net take home pay of all employees of the City is not below ₱4,000.00; 
 
Refrain from signing loan applications if the mandated net take home pay of the employee is below ₱4,000.00; and
 
Authorize deductions only if the loan was obtained from accredited financing institutions.
observations.managementAction: The management said that RATA entitle was monitored in accordance with the CSC regulations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor reiterates her directives on the timely submission of employees’ DTRs within the first week of the succeeding month to support the payroll by issuing another memorandum for strict compliance.  

We also recommended that the City Accountant discontinue the processing of DVs for payment of monthly salaries of individuals who fail to comply with the submission of DTRs on time, except for valid reasons.

We recommended for strict compliance with the rules on claims of RATA. Management must direct the City Accountant to require the attachment to RATA payrolls of the necessary supporting papers for said claims to be accurate in amounts and valid such as:

Certified list of officers entitled to RATA with liquidation as to the number of actual attendance in office and the days with approved application leave.
Cetified list of officers entitled to RATA  but adopt a work schedule other than the usual eight hours per day or forty hours per week such as member of the local sanggunian with indication of percentage of number of sessions attended in relation to the total number of sessions conducted in month.
observations.managementAction: The management said that RATA entitle was monitored in accordance with the CSC regulations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management remit in full the amount due to GSIS within the prescribed period otherwise, appropriate penal sanctions may be imposed upon the City Officials for violating pertinent provisions of RA 928. In addition Accountant must maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of GSIS deductions from the Salaries of employees of the City. This would facilitate reconciliation and monitoring of the unremitted balances due to GSIS

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the necessary correcting
entries to record the projects in the appropriate asset accounts in order to present
the correct balance of the assets of the City. If the City Government intends to
donate such assets, the same may be transferred to the recipient barangays upon
their acceptance, supported with the necessary documents to effect their transfer
from the books of the City Government to the recipient barangays.

We recommended that the City Accountant identify and evaluate all
differences existing between the records of the City and its depository bank. Also,
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the errors affecting the Cash-in-
Bank account to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and communicate
to the bank any errors made by it, if any.

We recommended that the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant
obligate and record a transaction as accounts payable only when it is founded on a
valid claim and properly supported with sufficient evidence such as, but not limited
to, approved Disbursement Voucher, Purchase Order, Contracts, etc.

We further recommended that the City Accountant perform the following:
a) supervise closely the accounting staff and instruct them to follow strictly
the guidelines in recording the transactions to avoid blind certifications.
b) prepare the list of Accounts Payable that has been dormant for two years
or more and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in order to
reflect the correct balance of the account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant trace back the unreconciled
amount, conduct periodic monitoring of the payable accounts and designate
accounting personnel dedicated to maintaining an updated monthly schedule of
payables to support the amount reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant record depreciation of an asset
starting when it is available for use. Also, prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to
correct the Equity and Accumulated Depreciation accounts to reflect the accurate
balances of the affected accounts in the agency’s year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
4. Coordinate with the Source Agency concerned to determine and confirm the
accuracy of the amounts reflected in the Due to LGU and Due to NGA
accounts;
5. Identify the fund transfers wherein the intended programs/projects have
been completed, terminated, cancelled or abandoned in order to identify
which funds are due to be returned;
6. Prepare and update schedules on the composition of the Due to LGU and Due
to NGA accounts to support the book balances. Prepare adjusting entries to
reclassify recording of erroneous fund transfers.

We also recommended that the City Treasurer determine the availability of
cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in
favor of the Source Agencies to return all unutilized amount. In case the source
agency can no longer be identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of
Treasury.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
3. Require the City Assessor to:
(d) conduct continuous ocular inspections at the barangay level to
validate the actual use of the real properties;
(e) update the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties,
the exact addresses and identity of declared owners, the exemptions
on RPT, if any;
(f) update continuously the City’s records particularly on the Tax
Declarations issued and check if there are clerical errors due to
General Revisions.
4. Direct the City Treasurer to:
(f) develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign program to intensify
and maximize collection of RPT/SET Receivables;
(g) deputize bonded Barangay Treasurers, to collect all taxes on real
property located in the Barangay;
(h) prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies and post
quarterly the said list at the City Hall and in a publicly accessible and
conspicuous place in each barangay;
(i) issue a notice of delinquency to each concerned property owner;
(j) submit the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies, with a
statement of the reasons for such non-payment, to the Sanggunian
concerned on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year, with
a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for
collection either by administrative action through levy on real
property or by judicial action.
We also recommended that the City Mayor mandate every employee of the
City to coordinate with and assist the City Treasurer in her campaign to increase
collections of real property taxes.

We recommended that the Local Development Council identify specific
projects to be funded from the 20% DF, including the use of continuing
appropriations, based on priority needs of the City. Also, stop lump sum
appropriations as well as frequent augmentations, so that projects in the AIP of the
City will be implemented fully within the year.

We recommended that the City Mayor execute a MOA with the recipient
barangays for assistance in kind extended to said barangays incorporating in the MOA
the submission of requirements, such as proof of actual utilization of the materials for the
intended projects within a reasonable time after receipt of the materials. The MOA
should also require that the cost of materials granted by the City be recorded by the
recipient barangays as additions to their assets.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher immediately to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF to the appropriate
account under the Trust Fund in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA
No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 to safeguard the resources against
possible misuse/misappropriation.

We recommended that the City Mayor, through the CDRRMO, utilize the
Mitigation Fund of its LDRRMF solely for disaster- and calamity related purposes
only. This is without prejudiced to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately with the latter
any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of
the GSIS to tally with the City records on premium contributions.

We also recommended that the City’s Agency Authorized Officer (AAO)
furnish monthly all payroll preparers with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the
correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in
70
the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.

Lastly, we recommended that the City Accountant reconcile monthly the
actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount
remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the City Treasurer issue checks for the barangays’
share of RPTs in the names of the Barangays concerned to ensure that such monies
will be deposited to the accounts of the barangays.

We recommended that the LSB incorporate in the SEF budget programs for
health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young
children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
1. Ensure that the net take home pay of all employees of the City is not
below ₱4,000.00;
2. Refrain from signing loan applications if the mandated net take home pay
of the employee is below ₱4,000.00; and
3. Authorize deductions only if the loan was obtained from accredited
financing institutions.

We recommended that the City Mayor reiterates her directives on the timely
submission of employees’ DTRs within the first week of the succeeding month to
support the payroll by issuing another memorandum for strict compliance.

We also recommended that the City Accountant discontinue the processing
of DVs for payment of monthly salaries of individuals who fail to comply with the
submission of DTRs on time, except for valid reasons.

We recommend that the City Assessor submit records to the Treasury for the latter to prepare the required Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card from the City Treasurer who shall thereafter, every beginning of any year, furnish the Accountant with the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. Said list shall be the basis of the setting up of receivable accounts.
We recommend that contracts of job order workers should contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent term to serve as standard with which to base the actual accomplishment.
observations.reason: Verificati on disclosed that the detailed statement of duties and functions of JOs were not indicated in the contract of services.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for strict compliance with the rules on claims of RATA. Management must direct the City Accountant to require the attachment to RATA payrolls of necessary supporting papers for said claims to be accurate in amounts and valid such as: Certified list of officers entitled to RATA with indication as to the number of actual attendance in office and the days with approved application for leave. Certified list of officers entitled to RATA but adopt a work schedule other than the usual eight hours per day or forty hours per week such as member of the local sanggunian with indication of percentage of number of sessions attended in relation to the total number of sessions conducted in a month.
observations.reason: Verificati on disclosed that payment of RATA in the subsequent months were not supported with documents as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management remit in full the amount due to GSIS within the prescribed period otherwise, appropriate penal sanctions may be imposed upon the City officials for violating pertinent provisions of RA 9281. In addition, the City Accountant must maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of GSIS deductions from the salaries of employees of the City. This would facilitate reconciliation and monitoring of the unremitted balances due to GSIS.
observations.reason: Verificati on disclosed that remittance is not in full as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for management to cause the remittance in full of the amount withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.
observations.reason: Verificati on disclosed that taxes withheld were not remitted in full in the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the BAC to resort to public bidding unless the adoption of alternative mode is justified by existing conditions. In all subsequent procurement, conduct of bidding shall be made part of the agency’s plan before proceeding to the adoption of the prescribed alternative modes. In case of "shopping", we recommend for the coursing of all procurement of common-used supplies to the PS-DBM and if without the required stocks, have the pertinent Purchase Order stamped by said office as "no available stocks". Once duly complied with, shopping directly from outside suppliers may be done but only if situations will qualify for a recourse thereto.

We recommend for coursing of all procurement of common-used supplies to the PS-DBM and if without the required stocks, have the pertinent Purchase Order stamped by said office as "no available stocks". Once duly complied with, public bidding shall be adopted or shopping directly from outside suppliers but only if the situation will qualify for a recourse to said mode of procurement pursuant to the procurement law and its implementing rules.

We reiterate our recommendation that the City Mayor re-constitute  the
Inventory Team and mandate the conduct  of  a  complete  physical  inventory
within a specific period, prepare and submit the RPCPPE.  We also  recommend
that the City Accountant reconcile the  balances  of  the  RPCPPE  with  the
Accounting Records and verify any discrepancies noted.

We  recommend  that  the  City  Accountant  analyze  the  accounts  to
determine the erroneous entries made  and  re-classify  the  errors  to  the
appropriate accounts to reflect the correct balances  of  the  Due  to  LGUs
account.  Also, prepare subsidiary ledgers  for  the  account  if  used  for
various purposes and record financial transactions in the correct accounts.

We recommend that the MDRRMO strictly adhere with the laws, rules  and
regulations  in  the  planning,  budgeting,  execution  and   recording   of
transactions under the LDRRMF and  utilize  the  fund  fully  to  adequately
equip the constituents in the preparation and management  of  disaster-risks
towards a resilient community.

We recommend that the  City  Mayor,  through  the  City  Planning  and
Development Officer submit explanations for the use of 20% Development Fund
for programs and activities  not  aligned  with  DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum
Circular  No.  2011-1  dated  April  13,  2011.   Henceforth,  refrain  from
utilizing the 20% development fund for unauthorized expenses.

We recommend that the City Budget Officer  and  the  Bids  and  Awards
Committee appropriate budgets of projects based on the amount  contained  in
the duly approved  Annual  Investment  Program  (AIP).   Award  and  execute
contracts for infrastructure projects with  reference  to  the  appropriated
amounts to such projects.

We recommend that the Local School Board, through the  City  Mayor  as
Chairman, appropriate and utilize the Special Education  Fund  strictly  for
programs, projects and activities that  are  explicitly  authorized  in  the
guidelines for the use of the  SEF.   Also,  give  utmost  priority  on  the
improvement of school facilities in order to  provide  the  maximum  benefit
from the SEF.

We recommend that the City Mayor  require  each  concerned  Department
Head to fast-track the implementation of programs  and  projects  funded  by
National Government Agencies in accordance with  the  timeline  provided
for in the MOA.   For  subsequent  fund  transfers,  require  the  concerned
Department Head to obtain copies of duly approved and  notarized  MOAs  from
Source Agencies upon receipt of the fund.   Also,  require  them  to  submit
the  Liquidation  Reports  and  other  documents  to   the   Source   Agency
immediately upon completion of the projects.

We recommend and management agreed that for similar transactions,  the
Local Chief Executive or her representative request  the  source  agency  to
transfer funds intended for other LGUs directly  to  their  respective  bank
account. Also, for subsequent  fund  transfers,  strictly  observe  all  the
provisions contained  in  the  MOA  and  ensure  that  its  liquidation  are
supported with complete necessary documents.

We recommend that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee  (BAC)
instruct   the   BAC   Secretariat,   to   submit   copies   of    perfected
contracts/purchase orders to the Audit  Team  within  five  (5)  days  after
execution/issuance,  together  with   the   required   complete   supporting
documents.   Also, the BAC Chairman  to  monitor  its  submission  to  avoid
suspensions in audit.

We recommend that the City Government require  all  department  heads  to
stop the practice of  submitting  requisitions  on  a  piecemeal  basis  but
strictly follow  the   period  and  mode  of  procurement  provided  in  the
approved APP.  Also, the BAC Chairman to adopt competitive  bidding  in  all
procurement activities and resort  to  alternative  methods  of  procurement
only after the conditions required thereon are complied.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant
and the City Treasurer to issue checks to each barangay  equivalent  to  the
amount  of  their  respective  RPT  shares  in  prior  years.   Also,  remit
quarterly to the barangays any amount collected from RPT in  the  succeeding
years.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to strictly
remit, in full, all taxes withheld, within the prescribed due  date  to  the
Bureau of  Internal  Revenue  (BIR).   Surcharges  and  penalties  for  late
remittance shall be a personal liability of the employee.

We recommend that  the  City  Mayor  adopt  guidelines  to  safeguard,
operate and maintain proper storage of  motor  vehicles,  and  consider  the
inclusion of the construction of a covered garage in its  Annual  Investment
Plan in CY 2018.  Likewise, ensure the timely renewal of  registration  with
LTO and insurance of  all  its  motor  vehicles,  supported  with  necessary
documents required by law, so as not to hamper  its  operations,  and  avoid
the risk of unrecovered damages in case of accidents.

We recommend that the City Mayor, through the Solid  Waste  Management
Officer, strictly enforce penalties for violators of the provisions  of  the
City Ordinances.  Also,  if  the  finances  of  the  LGU  warrants,  acquire
additional compactors, garbage trucks, other equipment and install  MRFs  in
the barangay level to achieve the  objectives  of  the  City’s  solid  waste
management program.

We reiterate our recommendation that management strictly utilize the results of the physical count on PPE in reconciling all maintained records by both the accountant and GSO. Furthermore, in order to effectively undertake the reconciliation, implementation of the measures proposed in the previous year is to be followed.
observations.managementAction: Reconcilia tion between records of the GSO and the Accounting section is on-going.
observations.reason: Inadequate records maintained by the accounting and GSO section for the prior years’ acquired PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that perfected contracts together with the documents forming part thereof be furnished the Auditor of the agency within five (5) working days from their execution.

We recommend for the Accounting Section to ensure that all recorded transactions are properly supported with Journal Entry Vouchers and necessary supporting documents which will be submitted for audit to the office of the Auditor.

We recommend that management should increase their real property tax collection by: Employing available remedies stated in the Section 256 of the Local Government Code or RA 7160. Conducting tax campaigns in coordination with the provincial treasury office and the barangay officials to maximize tax collection. Sending notices of tax delinquency to taxpayers/land owners and shall state that personal property maybe distrained to effect payment (Sec. 254(b) of RA 7160).

We recommend management to: Prepare Liquidation Reports for submission to the Source Agency; and Transfer the unutilized and dormant trust funds to the Source Agency or General Fund wherever applicable through the Bureau of the Treasury.
We recommend for the submission of the lacking documents and henceforth, supporting documents should be complete to facilitate audit action and to establish validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommend that management strictly abide with the laws and rules on utilization of SEF to avoid audit suspension and disallowance in the process and achieve the objectives of the fund. We further recommend the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to correct the erroneous entry double recording of procured vehicle, in effect, reflect correct valuations in the financial statements.

Attach the following documents in the payment of hazard duty pay for verification and evaluation in the process to establish their validity/legality and propriety: list of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description that describes the basis for justifying such positions that are exposed to high risk or low risk in accordance of IRR of RA No. 7305. Duly accomplished time record of employees or travel report. Copy of special order from the agency/department head covering the assignment to hazardous/difficult areas. Approved DTR/Service Report.

We recommend that accounting section to correctly record all withheld business taxes under the appropriate income, whereas those business taxes withheld and recorded under the Trust Funds shall be transferred periodically to the General Fund as income for management to use the proceeds thereof specifically for development projects and activities.

We recommend that: a) Proper planning of participants should be done before the seminar is conducted; b) Management should see to it that participants confirm their attendance before they contract for the meals and snacks so that the budget will only be for the confirmed participants and funds will not be wasted; c) Office personnel should have an office order requiring them to attend the seminar as participants, resource persons or facilitators before their presence in the seminar will be counted as valid.
We recommend that financial assistance granted should be strictly utilized in accordance with the purpose stated in the Work and Financial Plan and the MOA.

Require the refund of the illegally disbursed additional compensation; Strictly assess the eligibility of personnel before undertaking grant of allowances. It shall refrain from paying allowances to employees that are not specifically allowed under a law.

Stop sending Job Order employees on official travels and consequently stop the payment of travelling expenses to Job Order employees. Henceforth, only government officials and employees on official travel will be granted the payment of travelling expenses in accordance with laws and regulations.
